rulings.Case Title: DANIEL L. BALSAM VS. DSG DIRECT INC., A FLORIDA CORPOATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc And Motion To Compel Further Responses And For Monetary Sanctions 3 Declarations And 6 Seperate Statements
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 10, 2011. Line 1. PLAINTIFF DANIEL BALSAM'S Motion To Compel Further Responses And For Monetary Sanctions. Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses as to Request For Production Of Documents as to all Judgment Debtors Granted. Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to Special interrogatories from Your-Info, Inc is Denied as to Numbers 2, 13, 15 to 21. Granted as to all other. Motion to compel further responses to Special Interrogatories as to TROPICINKS, LLC. Numbers 2 to 4, 19 to 22, 24 Denied. Granted as to all other. Motion to Compel Further responses to Special Interrogatories as to DSG DIRECT, INC., numbers 19 to 22 Denied. Granted as to all other. Judgment Debtors must provide verifications of all discovery responses and must file responses within 30 days of entry of this order. Motion for sanctions is Denied. = (302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: PETER HEIN VS. CITY COLLEGE OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 10, 2011, line 4, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO COMMUNITY COLLEGE DISTRICT MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT MET ITS SUMMARY JUDGMENT BURDEN BY SHOWING THAT IT HAD A LEGITIMATE REASON FOR NOT INTERVIEWING PLAINTIFF. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO PRESENT SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE TO PROVE THAT DEFENDANT'S DECISION WAS MOTIVATED BY RACE, GENDER, PLAINTIFF'S DISABILITY, OR A RETALIATORY MOTIVE. FURTHER PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS FOR DISABILITY DISCRIMINATION AND RETALIATION ARE UNTIMELY AND PLAINTIFF FAILED TO EXHAUST HIS ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES. THE ONLY FEHA CHARGES THAT REFERENCE THOSE ISSUES WERE FILED IN APRIL 2008 AND THE RIGHT TO SUE LETTER ON THOSE CLAIMS WERE ISSUED ON MAY 1, 2008. PLAINTIFF FILED THIS ACTION MORE THAN A YEAR LATER. THE RIGHT TO SUE LETTER ISSUED NOVEMBER 25, 2008 WAS BASED ON A CHARGE THAT ONLY REFERENCED RACIAL AND SEXUAL DISCRIMINATION. UNDER OKOLI V. LOCKHEED TECHNICAL OPERATIONS CO. (1995) 36 CAL.APP.4TH 1607. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO EXHAUST HIS ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: EKA EKANEM VS. MARKETTOOLS, INC., A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion To Compel Deft Further Responses And Production Of Pltf'S 1st Set Of Requests For The Production Of Documents; And For Costs And Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 10, 2011, line 3, PLAINTIFF EKA EKANEM'S Motion To Compel Defendant Further Responses And Production Of Plaintiff'S First Set Of Requests For The Production Of Documents; And For Costs And Fees. Plaintiff¿s Motion to Strike General Objections is denied. Plaintiff must meet and confer with opposing counsel on these issues. That being said, the Court will provide some guidance to the issues raised. First, to the extent that the Defendant has made representations that the Plaintiff¿s termination was due to a restructuring of the Accounting department because of economic considerations of the Company¿s overall general finances and profitability, information and documents related to these issues are discoverable subject to a Protective Order relating to personal information of Defendant¿s Employees and its financial information. Plaintiff¿s Motion to Compel Responses Request for Production of Document is granted in part and denied in part as follows: No.16. Granted. No.23. Granted. No. 24. Granted. No. 57. Denied. This request is duplicative of No. 56. Also, the relevant time period for responses should be between December 2008 through July 2010. Insofar as the documents contain personal information of other employees, such as home addresses, telephone numbers or social security numbers, that information may be redacted as well as personnel information such as performance reviews and disciplinary information/memos. No. 61. Granted No. 63. Granted. No. 66. Denied. This is duplicative of Request No. 63. No. 67. Granted in part, denied in part. This is overly broad. Defendant should only provide a listing of the other jobs eliminated or employees transferred during the applicable time period. No other employment information or identifying information except employee name and position need be provided. No. 70. Granted. No. 71. Denied. This is duplicative of 70. No. 72. Denied. This is duplicative of 70 and 71. No

rulings.Case Title: MAX ZARZANA VS. SAN FRANCISCO RESIDENTIAL RENT STABILIZATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Issuance Of Peremptory Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 10, 2011, line 13. PETITIONER MAX ZARZANA'S Motion For Issuance Of Peremptory Writ Of Mandate is denied. Petitioner has failed to meet his burden under the substantial evidence test. Review of the administrative records show that the A.L.J. had substantial evidence before it to conclude that petitioner had a special relationship with the owners and moved into the apartment because of that relationship. Second, there is no record that the petitioner raised the argument of illegally permitted banked increases below. Accordingly, it is not properly before this court. Even if it were, a reading of section 6.11(a)(2) does not support petitioner's argument. Third, there was sufficient evidence to support the rental rate determinations. Finally, petitioner was not entitled to an appeal of the A.L.J.'s recalculation of rent on remand. =(302/LMG)
